content: Learn how to flash your device, connect and run commands.
title: Getting Started

content: GhostESP offers scanning, monitoring, and portal tools that run on-device. Here you can find help on how to use these features.
title: Wi-Fi

content: GhostESP supports GPS modules for location tracking and wardriving. Capture wireless network data with GPS coordinates and upload to WiGLE for mapping and research.
description: Overview of GhostESP's GPS and wardriving capabilities
title: GPS & Wardriving

content:
GhostESP supports SubGHz workflows on builds with compatible RF hardware, including CC1101-based scanning, signal capture, replay of authorized captures, and spectrum visualization tools.
Use these features only on devices, signals, and environments you own or are explicitly authorized to test.
title: SubGHz

content: GhostESP includes Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) scanning and attack tools. Here you can find help on how to use these features.
title: Bluetooth LE

content:
GhostESP lets you learn IR signals, store them on the SD card, and retransmit them using the onboard LED.
Supported protocolsNEC / NECext Kaseikyo Pioneer RCA Samsung SIRC (12-bit, 15-bit, 20-bit) RC5 RC6 If a remote uses an unsupported protocol, GhostESP still attempts to capture raw timings so you can replay the signal.
title: Infrared

content:
The GhostESP Flipper Zero companion app provides a comprehensive graphical interface to control and interact with your GhostESP device directly from your Flipper Zero. This app enables you to perform Wi-Fi operations, Bluetooth scanning, GPS tracking, and more without needing a computer or web browser.
The companion app is a Flipper Application Package (.fap) that runs on Flipper Zero devices. It communicates with GhostESP firmware via serial/UART connection, providing an intuitive menu-driven interface for all major GhostESP features.
Repository: github.com/jaylikesbunda/ghost_esp_app
Quick LinksInstallation - Install the companion app on your Flipper Zero Connection Setup - Hardware and software connection guide Wi-Fi Features - Wi-Fi scanning, capture, and attacks BLE Features - Bluetooth scanning and attacks GPS Features - GPS tracking and wardriving Configuration - LED, SD card, and system settings Usage Guide - Common workflows and navigation Troubleshooting - Solve common issues
description: Control GhostESP from your Flipper Zero device using the official companion app.
title: Flipper Zero App

content:
GhostESP includes an NRF24 analyzer for viewing 2.4 GHz activity across channels 0-125.
Use this section for setup, local analyzer usage, and GhostLink remote control mode.
description: NRF24L01 frequency analyzer and GhostLink remote control
title: NRF24

content: GhostESP ships with a PN532-based reader that can scan, cache, save, and rewrite ISO14443A tags.
title: NFC

content: GhostESP includes app-style views that are meant to run interactively from the on-device menu. This section covers setup and usage for those apps.
description: Standalone apps and interactive tools available on GhostESP
title: Apps

content: GhostESP supports USB Host mode on ESP32-S3 devices, allowing you to connect USB peripherals like keyboards for input control.
description: USB Host features for ESP32-S3 devices
title: USB

content: GhostESP provides Ethernet connectivity with network scanning, device fingerprinting, and service discovery tools. Here you can find help on how to use these features.
title: Ethernet

content: Learn how to build GhostESP from source, adjust configurations, and contribute changes.
